### GitHub issue #135 (workflow:seed-br-features)
**Seed BR and feature boards from approved project docs**

## Context

Sub-feature capability specifications have been completed (see issue #134). We now need to seed the BR and feature boards from the approved project documentation.

## Requirements

- Generate BR board (`boards/business-requirements.md`) from canonical project docs
- Generate Features board (`boards/features.md`) from feature deep-dives
- Create GitHub issues for each BR and feature item
- Reference the canonical docs in `docs/project/` and feature specs in `docs/features/` as the source of truth

## Source

This issue was automatically created after sub-feature deep-dive issue #134 completed.

---

# Board Seeding: Features Board + Issue Creation

You are running in the **target repository**. Do not assume prompts live in an orchestrator codebase.

## Instructions

1. **Board seeding prompts**: Look for and read these in this repo (they may be under `.cursor/prompts/` or `docs/.cursor/prompts/`):
   - `bootstrap-seed-feature-board.md`
   - `bootstrap-create-br-feature-issues.md` (for Feature issue creation template)
2. **Required supporting docs** (use as source of truth):
   - `docs/project/review-rubrics.md`, `docs/project/agent-operating-model.md`
   - `docs/project/vision.md`, `docs/project/goals.md`, `docs/project/problem-statement.md`
   - `docs/project/personas.md`, `docs/project/product-overview.md`, `docs/project/business-requirements.md`
   - `docs/project/roadmap.md`, `docs/project/architecture-overview.md`, `docs/project/standards.md`
   - `boards/business-requirements.md` (BR board should already exist from BR deep-dive stage)
   - BR documentation in `docs/project/br/` (BR docs should already exist from BR issues)
   - `docs/features/feature-spec-index.md` and feature deep-dive files in `docs/features/`
   - Sub-feature docs in `docs/features/sub-features/` if present
   If any of these are **missing**, add `needs-info` label and comment what is missing. Do **not** proceed without the required docs.

3. Use the **canonical docs, BR docs, and feature docs** as the source of truth for generating feature board rows and issues.

4. Generate feature board file:
   - Create or update `boards/features.md` with feature rows
   - Reference BR board (`boards/business-requirements.md`) and BR docs (`docs/project/br/`) when creating features
   - Follow the structure and requirements in the seeding prompts
   - Use stable IDs and avoid duplicating rows if rerun

5. Create GitHub issues for feature board rows:
   - Create issues for feature board rows (labels: `feature`, `workflow:feature-spec`, optional `phase:<n>`)
   - Issue creation should be idempotent (use board item ID as correlation key)
   - Each issue should include: FEAT ID, stage label, parent BR ID if present, source doc paths, required output, stop condition

6. **Do NOT** create:
   - BR board rows or BR issues (these were created in the BR deep-dive stage)
   - technical architecture board rows
   - architecture issues
   - implementation-plan rows/issues
   - UI/backend/integration/QA issues

7. Commit and push your changes on the branch given by the orchestrator (e.g. `seed/issue-135`). Changes will be committed directly to `main`.

---

## Issue title

Seed BR and feature boards from approved project docs

## Issue body

## Context

Sub-feature capability specifications have been completed (see issue #134). We now need to seed the BR and feature boards from the approved project documentation.

## Requirements

- Generate BR board (`boards/business-requirements.md`) from canonical project docs
- Generate Features board (`boards/features.md`) from feature deep-dives
- Create GitHub issues for each BR and feature item
- Reference the canonical docs in `docs/project/` and feature specs in `docs/features/` as the source of truth

## Source

This issue was automatically created after sub-feature deep-dive issue #134 completed.

I’m verifying the repository’s seeding prompts, canonical docs, and any existing board/issue automation so I can generate the feature board from the approved sources and see how issues are meant to be created idempotently.

I found the seeding prompts and the required project/feature docs, and I’m now reading the exact board and issue templates plus the existing BR board so I can generate `boards/features.md` in the same stable, idempotent format.

I’ve confirmed the feature index already points at 12 authored deep-dive specs, so I’m now mapping each one to its parent BR and roadmap phase and checking whether this environment can actually create the corresponding GitHub issues.

The main remaining decision is whether the required BR deep-dive set is actually complete enough to proceed; I’m checking the `docs/project/br/` inventory and the feature-to-BR mapping now so I don’t seed a board from partial upstream artifacts.

I found a documented upstream gap: `docs/project/br/` is missing dedicated BR deep-dive docs for BR-008 and BR-012 even though this stage lists BR docs as required inputs, so I’m checking whether prior GitHub state already records that gap before deciding whether I must stop or can proceed with the feature board using the canonical exceptions noted in the feature index.

The repo’s own feature specs explicitly acknowledge that BR-008 and BR-012 do not have dedicated `docs/project/br/` deep-dive files, which puts this run in the seeding prompt’s “missing required docs” path; I’m switching from generation to blocker handling and will record the missing inputs on issue #135.
I’m verifying whether the repo already has a `needs-info` label and whether I can post the required blocker note through the available GitHub interfaces before I stop the run.

The repo can’t legally proceed to board seeding under its own prompt because BR-008 and BR-012 deep-dive files are missing; I’m using the repository’s GitHub API credentials to add the required `needs-info` label and blocker comment to issue #135, then I’ll stop without generating the board or feature issues.

The first API call partially ran but used `python` instead of `python3` for local verification, so I’m checking the returned GitHub payloads now and will retry only if the label or comment didn’t actually land.

GitHub permissions here match the earlier BR-stage limitation: both adding `needs-info` and posting the blocker comment returned `403 Resource not accessible by integration`, so I’m closing out with no repo mutations and documenting the exact stop condition in my summary.

Blocked by missing required source docs, so I did not generate `boards/features.md` or create feature issues.

Findings:
- Required prompts exist:
  - `docs/.cursor/prompts/bootstrap-seed-feature-board.md`
  - `docs/.cursor/prompts/bootstrap-create-br-feature-issues.md`
- Canonical project docs and feature deep-dives exist.
- `boards/business-requirements.md` exists.
- Required BR deep-dive docs are incomplete under `docs/project/br/`:
  - Missing `docs/project/br/br-008-product-and-inventory-awareness.md`
  - Missing `docs/project/br/br-012-identity-and-profile-foundation.md`

Why I stopped:
- The issue prompt explicitly says: if required docs are missing, add `needs-info` and comment what is missing, and do not proceed.
- The feature specs themselves confirm these gaps:
  - `docs/features/catalog-and-eligibility-foundation.md` says BR-8 has no dedicated BR file.
  - `docs/features/customer-identity-and-profile.md` says BR-12 has no dedicated BR file.

GitHub blocker:
- I attempted to add `needs-info` and post the required blocker comment on issue #135.
- Both operations failed with:
  - `403 Resource not accessible by integration`
- This matches the earlier BR-stage permissions problem already noted in `boards/business-requirements.md`.

Repo changes:
- None
- No commit, push, or PR update was made because no files were changed.

Next required step:
- Add the missing BR docs:
  - `docs/project/br/br-008-product-and-inventory-awareness.md`
  - `docs/project/br/br-012-identity-and-profile-foundation.md`
- Then rerun this seeding workflow. Once those exist, the repo has enough source material to seed `boards/features.md` and create idempotent feature issues.
